meta.note: Public deposit copy: participant given-name field removed. Identities may remain inferable from cited source posts (retained for auditability). Any individual may request removal of their case: jake@endmyopia.org.

cases.verification_quote: 26th January 2013 I had a free eye test at the opticians: Left: -4.50 RIght: -4.75
My starting “full strength” prescription was -5.5 -0.5 right eye/-5.0 -0.5 left eye. In September, the checkup showed -4.75 -0.25 for right eye/-4.25 -0.5 for the left.

cases.start_numbers: -2.25 right eye / -1.50 left eye with slight astigmatism
cases.end_numbers: right eye -2.00 (optician); now reading the -1.75 line at 20ft (home chart)
cases.verification_quote: Last visit to opticians said his right eye has improved to -2.00 which is what I also tested it at on a home snellen chart at 20ft.

cases.end_numbers: exam-reduced to -8.50/-9.50 with 20/20; later trial contacts -8.00 and -9.00 with reported perfect vision
cases.verification_quote: But during the follow up exam, the OD told me I was over-prescribed and dropped my prescription to 8.50/9.50. I had now 20/20 vision with that new prescription.

cases.end_numbers: -2.50 (clears 20/20 at exam); optometrist's own determination -3.00 to -3.25
cases.verification_quote: I cleared 20/20 with both my right and left eye (measure by themselves) with my cheap -2.50 glasses from Zenni optical.

My astigmatism has reduced significantly already according to my latest Optometrist visit. OS from 1cyl to .5cyl and OD from 1.25cyl to .5cyl. On the Sph side of things, I’m down about .5 to .75 diopters so far.
After two years, I went back to the optometrist. My prescription was -4.75. For the first time in my life, my eyes has improved!!

cases.end_numbers: -3.25 normalized (reads 20/20 with it); optometrist at start of year: -4.75 fully corrected
cases.verification_quote: At the start of this year I had a trip to the optometrist, and was told that my fully corrected prescription was -4.75.

cases.start_numbers: OD -2.50 *-.25 *177 OS -2.00 *-.50 *177 (previous normalized; original start not stated)
cases.verification_quote: The reduced prescription is: OD -3.25 -.50 177 OS -2.50 -.75 177 He said that the reduction was 1 diopter per eye.
He had ordered me a trial pair of -7.00 contacts (I guess he was not expecting much after I put them in). To his amazement I was able to get to 20/20 (without squinting) with the new lowered script.
[9/2014 I started improving my myopia.] 11/11/2015 – OD -6.00 Cyl -0.25 @ 035 – OS -6.50 Cyl -0.25 @ 137

cases.end_numbers: 20/20 on Snellen with -5 L / -6 R; new normalized ordered -4.75 L / -5.75 R
cases.verification_quote: I went to the eye doctor back in early February. I started this program early January. According to the tests, my left eye went from -6.5 to -6 and -7.5 down to -7.25.

cases.end_numbers: wearing OS -5.00D / OD -4.00D spherical, seeing 20/40 (20/25 or better in contacts, same script)
cases.verification_quote: So, after about ~15 months, my long-time OD verified a .75D reduction for both eyes to 20/15. While not spectacular, it is a measureable result that cannot be ignored even by my OD, who does not really believe in therapy.

cases.start_numbers: contacts Right: -8.00 cyl -0.75 axis 010 / Left: -8.50 cyl -0.75 axis 180
cases.verification_quote: I went to the optometrist to get a formal exam and see the progress I made, which was in July 2015. The results are: Contact lens- Right: -7.50 (no cyl correction) Left: -8.00 (no cyl correction)
Starting Rx [Feb 2014] OS -7.50d, -1c | OD -6.00d, -1.25c Optometrist [May 2015] OS -6.75d, -1c | OD -5.25d, -1c
Stefanie’s full prescription is down by a half diopter, as measured by her own optometrist. She’s legal to drive with lenses 1.5 diopters lower than her original full prescription.

After just under a month of wearing my normalized prescription, my cm have increased by 2cm in each eye!!! And I am thrilled!!! Because that’s 0.50 Dioptre in my right eye and 0.75 Dioptre in my left.
I have today just came back from the optometrist with a lower proscription (-3.75) I can’t believe it it’s Amazing!!!!

people not listening to their retina specialist about further increasing a -3 diopter prescription – and then coming back and having the very same establishment confirm 20/30 with *zero* lens correction
Starts with a -4 in 2013, gets down to a -3, no high hopes for going even lower, but ends up with a -2 for his official eye exam, this year!
Rock ‘n roll, Andrew. 20/20 gains, grudgingly (but without curiosity) confirmed by the optometrist.
I returned to my optometrist and demanded a new refraction. I was able to get a -5.25/-5.75 prescription. My optometrist agreed to remove the remaining cylinder at my request.
-5.00 down to -3.00. That’s a 40% reduction in myopia, and confirmed by the optometrist.
April 2017 (optometrist confirmed) Right -1.50 no cyl Left -1.25 no cyl Spectacle adjusted.
Optometrist Confirmed:  Jayesh Reduced -8.50 D To -7.00 D
Kristen:  From -4.25 To -3.25 (In Two Months)

cases.end_numbers: -4.25 L (no astigmatism) / -5 R (cyl -0.5), optometrist trial-lens exam Sept 2017
cases.verification_quote: By the time we were done, my final “full” prescription was actually -4.25(L) with NO astigmatism and -5(R) with -0.5 astigmatism.
Aniket: -6.75 Down To -4.50 (Optometrist Confirmed)
Nicole: Ophthalmologist Confirms -3.5 D Myopia Reduction
At his next appointment with our pediatrician, just a week later, his eyesight was 20/20.
Here’s Ani, and 20/20 gains, and results quantified by the optometrist: Nicely done. A full diopter in 2.5 months, pretty good.
Dave: -4.50 Down To -2.50 (Optometrist Confirmed)
Megan: -10 Down To -6 (Optometrist Confirmed) | Shortsighted Podcast
Then I went back to be examined in October of this year and was reduced to -3.00 for distance!

I’ve attached results from my 1st biometry from around 1 year ago and then the results I’ve just gotten today. Looks like there’s been a measurable change in the axial length of the eye reducing respectively by 0.08 mm in the left eye and a whole 0.1 mm in the right eye.
Pedro: -3.00 To -0.50 Diopter Progress

cases.start_numbers: about 1 to 1,5 diopters (maximum/fallback state; a little more on the left eye)
cases.verification_quote: Roughly one year and one period of losing and regaining interest further, today I did a further eye test, which confirms my roughly halved numbers of low myopia.
Here’s Chris, having this experience: How much more official does it get, than that, as far as quantified vision improvement?
Back To 20/20: Emily Passes DMV Vision Test
Uncorrected driving license. Win.

First Jordan, who despite having fallen off the 20/20 gains wagon for a bit, has managed some significant milestones: On a positive note. If the state says you don’t need glasses to drive, then you pretty well don’t need glasses.
Srinidhi:  Passed Drivers License Test, NO GLASSES
Brenda: -2.00 To DMV Passed Is there proof-ier proof than the government telling you that you’re fine to drive without corrective lenses?
Drivers License Restriction Removed (Bonus: Presbyopia Improved)
DMV Restriction Removed, Presbyopia Reversed (Improved 60%)

cases.start_numbers: minus 3.00 in one eye (with minus 0.50 cyl) and minus 2.00 in the other
cases.verification_quote: These two images represent what my naked-eye vision was like before and after 18 months of hard work on improving my vision.
Till early this year, I enjoyed several years of excellent eyesight with no glasses. 20/12 would be a normal measurement, in good daylight.
Just quickly took a cm measurement today while doing some focusing. So I can read a book clearly at 34cm now..so have improved from 30/31cm.
About 3 months ago, I had -7.75 diopters of myopia and -7 diopters in my right, according to the centimeter calculator.
I have also been keeping track of centimeter measurements. Beginning of program, August 9, morning, R 28 cm L 17 cm August 10, morning R 24 cm L 15 cm September 6, today, morning R 40 cm L 18 cm

Within a couple of weeks into the program and armed with the Snellen chart I discovered that she was overprescribed by about -0.5D. I then changed her glasses to the reduced power and also started following the recommendations in the program.
I took my old 2002 (-3.25D, -2.75) “real” prescription and I test my vision with my 20 feet “not so well lightened” Snellen and realise that I can now, easily, without any effort, read 20/15 !
I wore glasses for 20 years and I just read 20/20 in about a month of vision recovery.

cases.start_numbers: -2,5 left / -2,25 right (prescribed about a year before starting)
cases.verification_quote: At day one I measured 46cm (at the evening) on the myopia calculator and I could barely work at the computer without glasses. Now 30 days later I can do (at 11:00 am) 58cm with my right eye and 52cm with my left eye.

Okay, right now it’s a bit more than 30 days (thanks to some off days), and we have had some off days, but some of his progress has been pretty great. Here are his measurements from day 1: Left Eye 21cm -4.75 Worst 20/400 Best 20/100

cases.end_numbers: -1.75 (current lens, reads 20/20 most of the time; cm 49-52)
cases.verification_quote: With this -1.75 if the lighting is really good I can read the 20/20 line. Most of the time lately I have read the 20/20 line successfully. This morning, when it was really sunny, I read the 20/15 line almost perfectly.

I’ve been doing the program for a year now and while I’ve noticed improvements in the vision I can achieve (measured on the snellen chart) with both prescriptions, my vision without glasses remains the same at 11cm/12cm.

cases.end_numbers: OD -3.5 / OS -4.0 (from cm measurement; wearing -3.75 glasses)
cases.verification_quote: My current measurements: myopia calculator: OD 28cm / OS 25cm which translates into: OD -3.5 / OS -4.0

Those numbers, especially involving the differential prescriptions, don’t lie. Some of what I’ve experienced could be classified as wishful thinking to some extent, but 62 to 90 cm (with the -3 glasses) is an obvious, definite difference.

When I started, I could read 20/30 line reliably, and could sometimes make out the 20/25 or even 20/20 without much trouble using glasses with a -8.25 prescription. Now, I can read the 20/20 line with that prescription almost every time

cases.start_numbers: Right eye -6.25, left eye -7.00 with -0.50 x 165 astigmatism (left)
cases.verification_quote: My snellen measurements for day 23 were: right – 23cm, left – 21cm, both – 25cm in the morning and right – 21cm, left – 19cm, both – 24cm after reading and using the computer for a few hours.

cases.start_numbers: R: 7.5 and L: 6.5 (distance, minus sign omitted in text)
cases.verification_quote: I received my contact lenses today and decided to try them out. I was surprised how much more they improved my vision and decided to do a Snellen test. I was surprised that I could read the 20/13 line at a distance of about 5.20 m

cases.start_numbers: -4.75 -0.75 x 90 contacts both eyes (Feb 2014); glasses peak OD -5.00 -1.50 x 85 / OS -5.50 -1.00 x 120 (2006)
cases.verification_quote: I have been wearing the -3.00 0.75 x 90 prescription for about 3 weeks now and my eyesight has improved over that time but is still not 20/20. I have a snellen and good reference points in the distance at work.
Day 2 with Normalized, 20 foot Snellen: Right 20/40 Left 20/30 Both 20/25 Week 5 with Normalized, 20 foot Snellen: Right 20/25 Left 20/20 Both 20/20

I can see most of the letters on the 20/15 line with -1.5 lenses, 20feet snellen. All measurements taken without glasses. Centimeters from Snellen chart to unreadable 20/20 line * Left: 140 * Right: 156 * Both: 166
i measured my centimeters and they were 33/29, which is clearly better than the previous peak of 32/26 or so a month ago
Today: Contacts: Right: -3.50 Left: -4.00 20/20 each eye inside, 20/15 or better both eyes open.
I’m in month 2, measuring cm’s for the normalized prescription. Here’s what’s happened so far: Starting point: (contacts) R: -5.0 L: -5.25 Starting cm’s: R: 20 L: 18.5

cases.start_numbers: -4.75 -0.75 x 90 (contacts); peak spectacles OD -5.00 -1.50 x 85, OS -5.50 -1.00 x 120
cases.verification_quote: To recap it has been 18 months since I started my rehab and I have reduced my prescription from about -5.00 SPH -1.50 CYL to 1.75 SPH in both eyes.
my centimeter has gone from about 7.5-8.0 cm in each eye to 9.0 cm
I’ve put the -4.00s in, as a laugh, to see how blurry the world would be… And I read read 20/20 on the Snellen. I can even make out 20/15. This is with fresh eyes and natural lighting

cases.start_numbers: R -5.00 contact (20 cm) / L -5.25 contact (17 cm)
cases.end_numbers: -3.00 contact both eyes (R 27.5 cm, 20/25; L 25.5 cm, 20/30)
cases.verification_quote: Day 1 cm: R: 20 (-5.00 contact) L: 17 (-5.25 contact) Day 75 starting patching( 15min left eye) at: R: 27cm –> -3.00 contact: easy 20/25 L: 24cm –> -3.25 contact: hard 20/50
At the time, his prescription was -1.25/1.00. His centimer measurement was 90. After 9 months of work, his centimeter is 175, and on the snellen he gets to 20/30.
I actually can see the 20/20 line after two weeks in -5.75 contacts (converts to -6.0 glasses). When I first started, I was pulling 20/50 and 20/40, and now I’m pulling 20/30 20/20 with effort

cases.end_numbers: -7.5 contacts in both eyes (already reduced), asking to try -7
cases.verification_quote: Since starting the program 6 weeks ago, I spend 20 minutes every morning without my glasses and take the cm measurements after. My cm have improved from about 11 cm initially to almost 13 (left 12-12.5, right 12.5-13.2)
I was prescribed -4.75 in December last year. In good light, I can now read the 20/20 line with -3.00.

It has been about 5 month now since I have started applying the principles of your method and the results are nothing short of amazing. I can now see nearly 20/10 in fluorescent lighting in -1.25L/ -1.5R

cases.start_numbers: Sept 12, 2015: OD -9.75 +025 sph / OS -10.25 +.25 sph (glasses); contacts OU -8.00
cases.verification_quote: Now I have been wearing the new normalized for 3 1/2 weeks. I could read to the 20/30 on the snellen this morning in low light, almost 20/20 in sunlight
I’ve been wearing the -1.00 for 2 weeks now and I am getting very good vision, double vision nearly gone and close to 20/20 these past few days

cases.start_numbers: Normalized L -8.00 / R -7.75 (2016-01-25); original 2011 prescription L -8.50 -0.50 180 / R -8.00
cases.verification_quote: This is an improvement from the 20/85 vision I had in spring time, and when I put on my new normalized with -6.25, everything looks amazing, even with gray skies above.

cases.start_numbers: January 2013: L sph -1.75 cyl -0.75 180, R sph -2.5 cyl -0.75 10

cases.end_numbers: -3.75 L / -4.25 R (late January 2016; just ordered -3.5 L / -4 R)
cases.verification_quote: After resolving some double vision , I managed to drop down to -3.75(L), 4.25(R) late January 2016.

cases.start_numbers: prescribed -1.25 lenses (never worn); reading 20/50 line on Snellen
cases.verification_quote: The last time I tested him he read the entire 20/25 line, and got 2 letters right on the 20/20 line, which is pretty good, although not perfect.

cases.end_numbers: -3.00 (cm-based; Jake estimates -3.75 'to be on the safe side')
cases.verification_quote: I’d call it a -3.75, just to be on the safe side. That’s still a staggering three diopters, or basically cutting the prescription in half, in less than half a year.
I’m exactly 30 days in to a new normalized of L -1.25 / R -1.75 (down from L -1.50 / R -2.00). Just yesterday, this prescription “clicked” for me.
I stopped wearing contact lenses in mid march so it took me roughly 2 years and 1 month to go from -5.50 in each eye to where I am now

cases.start_numbers: -5.5 in both eyes (normalized May 5/16: L -5 R -5; cm 18-19)
cases.verification_quote: May 5/16 Normalized L: -5 R: -5 Differential L: -3.75 R: -3.75 CM measure: 18-19 cm june 3/16 Normalized L: -4.5 R-4.5 Differential L: -3.5 R: -3.5 CM measure: 21 cm

I’ve been experimenting with different strengths and have found that with; L: -2.00 R: -1.50 – things seem perfect, with both eyes is at 20/20 very easily on the Snellen – at 20/15 right and 20/20 left. That’s a 0.50 reduction in both eyes

My Centimeters have gone from averaging 13/14 to 15/17 to 17/18. My prescription have gone in the following sequence original prescription od -6.25 -1.25 x 20 os -7.50 -0.50 x 165 first normalised od -5.25 -1.25 x20 os -6.5 -0.50 x165 second normalised od -5.25 -0.50 x20 os -6.25
almost 30 days into a new normalized of L -1.25 / R -1.50

Starting at 45 centimeter, going to a current 49 centimeter. It’s all there, the answers you’re looking for. Barb started out with way too much correction – she measured 45 cm which equals -2.25 diopter, a full diopter less than she’d been using

cases.start_numbers: glasses -5 / -6 D (worn, under-prescribed; stated actual myopia -6 / -7 D; +1.0 cylinder in glasses)
cases.verification_quote: Glasses became sharper than needed for computer work. In fact, on sunny days, I could see 20/20 on Snellen with them. So recently I changed them to -4.75/-5.75. Same for contact lenses, I switched to 0.25 weaker, as the previous ones felt like 20/20 most of the time.

cases.start_numbers: contact lens R -5.00, L -5.50 (about -5.50 / -6.00 spectacle), plus a bit of astigmatism
cases.verification_quote: Two days ago, I am thrilled to report, after a good 20 minutes of no prescription on, I popped in my 4th normalized contact lens prescription of R -3.25, L-3.75.
Today it is 12 months since I joined Endmyopia “Back to 20/20”, so it is a good time to look back at my progress. My prescription at that point was L-5.75, -2.25 and R -8.50, -2.25.

cases.end_numbers: normalized R -2.00 / L -1.50; cm R 38.6, L 47.6, B 48.7; Snellen 20/30 each eye
cases.verification_quote: my last three day centimeter testing (6/22-24) was my highest recording yet (R 38.6, L 47.6, B 48.7). The Snellen was good too, being at 20/30 for each eye.

I started at -3.75 full correction for both eyes. Right now I’m down to -1.5 normalized and full correction being around -2.0. I can see 20/40 easily with good lighting and with some active focus 20/30 is sometimes clear.
